mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rates signal deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce corporate earnings visibility, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Unemployment increases by >0.5pp but 20-day returns exceed 1.5% in 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and retail sales signals strong consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through increased corporate revenue expectations.
failure_condition: Both HOUST and RSAFS rise but 20-day returns are negative in >40% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates improving manufacturing activity, which boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases by >1.5% but 20-day returns are negative in >30% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 2.5% erodes real returns on equities, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors demand higher risk premiums.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL exceeds 237.92 but 5-day returns exceed 1.0% in 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth indicates robust labor market conditions that support consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS increases by >100k but 20-day returns are negative in >35% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP and housing starts signals expanding demand, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through increased corporate investment and consumption.
failure_condition: Both GDPC1 and HOUST rise but 60-day returns are negative in >40% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Monetary tightening cycles, indicated by rising federal funds rates, increase discount rates and reduce equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS increases by >0.3pp but 20-day returns exceed 1.0% in 60% of cases.
